stem: A urban planning study measures the commute time (minutes) for a sample of 30 city workers. The sample mean is 66.1 min with a sample standard deviation of 21.4 min. However, 2 of the 30 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 28 observations have a mean of 62.0 min and standard deviation of 13.5 min. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 66.1, 95% CI = [58.1, 74.1]. Without outliers: mean = 62.0, 95% CI = [56.9, 67.1]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [56.9, 74.1]. Point estimate: 64.0 min.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [56.9, 74.1].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
